categories.title: Product & UX
categories.description: Improve the path from first interaction to useful product value.

categories.title: Design & Prototyping
categories.description: Turn visual direction into a coherent, reviewable experience.

categories.title: Research & Decisions
categories.description: Convert evidence into decisions while keeping sources and assumptions visible.

categories.title: Content & Messaging
categories.description: Create focused messages and reusable content from approved source material.

categories.title: Data & Evaluation
categories.description: Inspect evidence, quantify quality, and explain what the results support.

categories.title: Operations & Support
categories.description: Turn operational knowledge into clear, usable workflows and support coverage.

goals.title: First Value Sprint
goals.summary: Find and remove the biggest obstacle between a new user and the product's first useful result.
goals.defaultGoal: Walk through the first-time user journey, identify the single biggest barrier to reaching value, make the smallest credible improvement, and prove the journey is clearer on desktop and mobile.

requiredInputs.label: Product surface
requiredInputs.description: The local app, prototype, screenshots, or flow to review.

requiredInputs.label: First value event
requiredInputs.description: The user action that proves the product became useful.

goals.sampleInput: A new user should create their first project and understand what to do next without assistance.
goals.expectedArtifact: A focused product change or review-ready recommendation with before-and-after journey evidence.

criteria: The first value event is stated in observable terms.
The highest-impact friction point is supported by inspected evidence.
The revised journey completes at desktop and mobile widths without introducing a new dead end.

reliability.summary: An experimental loop goal for one bounded onboarding improvement; it still requires product review.

goodExamples: reduce friction before a new user creates their first project
make the first value moment easier to reach on mobile
review this onboarding flow and fix its biggest dead end
badExamples: run a broad feature roadmap workshop

goals.title: Mobile Journey Rescue
goals.summary: Repair the first mobile usability blocker in one important product journey.
goals.defaultGoal: Complete the primary journey on a narrow mobile screen, repair the first blocking usability issue, and prove the important controls remain readable, reachable, and keyboard accessible.

requiredInputs.label: Primary journey
requiredInputs.description: The user path and final action that must work on mobile.

requiredInputs.label: Product surface
requiredInputs.description: The local app, prototype, or screenshots to inspect.

goals.sampleInput: A user must select a loop, customize the goal, and open the generated prompt from a phone-sized viewport.
goals.expectedArtifact: One scoped mobile usability fix with responsive and accessibility evidence.

criteria: The primary action is visible and usable without horizontal scrolling.
Text and controls do not clip, overlap, or become unreachable.
Keyboard focus and accessible labels remain clear for the changed interaction.

reliability.summary: A focused experimental goal for one journey, not a full responsive redesign.

goodExamples: fix the first blocker in checkout on a phone
make the goal picker usable at mobile width
repair the clipped primary action in this responsive flow
badExamples: create native iOS and Android apps

goals.title: Visual Consistency Pass
goals.summary: Find and repair the most visible inconsistency against an existing interface language.
goals.defaultGoal: Compare the selected interface with the product's established visual patterns, repair the most visible inconsistency, and provide restrained before-and-after evidence.

requiredInputs.label: Target interface
requiredInputs.description: The screen, component, or local route to improve.

requiredInputs.label: Reference language
requiredInputs.description: Existing screens, tokens, or brand guidance that define the intended direction.

goals.sampleInput: Align the loop-goal cards with the site's calm Vercel-inspired controls and spacing.
goals.expectedArtifact: One reviewable interface improvement with before-and-after visual evidence.

criteria: The change reuses established product tokens and interaction patterns.
The repaired inconsistency is visible in before-and-after evidence.
The result remains coherent at desktop and mobile widths.

reliability.summary: A bounded visual-polish goal that depends on a real reference interface.

goodExamples: make this card match the rest of the product
repair inconsistent spacing and controls on this screen
align this page with our existing visual language
badExamples: invent a complete brand from nothing

goals.title: Clickable Flow Prototype
goals.summary: Turn a clear workflow idea into the smallest clickable interface that can be reviewed.
goals.defaultGoal: Build the smallest clickable version of the supplied user flow, keep it consistent with the existing product language, and prove the primary path can be completed without explanation.

requiredInputs.label: User job
requiredInputs.description: The one outcome the prototype must help a user complete.

requiredInputs.label: Flow boundary
requiredInputs.description: The starting state, final state, and anything explicitly out of scope.

goals.sampleInput: Let a user choose a loop goal, review its proof criteria, and open the populated prompt in Codex.
goals.expectedArtifact: A local clickable prototype covering one primary path and its essential states.

criteria: The prototype covers one complete path from entry to outcome.
Every visible control performs a real action or communicates its disabled state.
A reviewer can identify and complete the primary action without additional explanation.

reliability.summary: An experimental goal for one reviewable flow; it does not imply production readiness.

goodExamples: build a clickable onboarding concept for one user path
turn this workflow sketch into a reviewable local prototype
make a small interaction prototype for selecting and launching a prompt

goals.title: Feedback Theme Synthesis
goals.summary: Turn a bounded set of feedback into traceable themes and one evidence-backed next decision.
goals.defaultGoal: Analyze the supplied feedback, group recurring themes, cite the source item behind every conclusion, and recommend one bounded next action without inventing customer evidence.

requiredInputs.label: Feedback set
requiredInputs.description: A bounded collection of interview notes, survey answers, or support items with stable IDs.

requiredInputs.label: Decision context
requiredInputs.description: The product decision this research should inform.

goals.sampleInput: Synthesize twelve numbered onboarding comments to decide which activation problem to address first.
goals.expectedArtifact: A concise research brief with themes, source references, confidence, and one recommended next action.

criteria: Every theme cites at least one supplied source item.
Observed evidence, interpretation, and assumptions are visibly separated.
The recommended action follows from the strongest theme and names remaining uncertainty.

reliability.summary: A traceable synthesis goal for bounded local feedback, not live customer intake.

goodExamples: find themes in these numbered customer interview notes
synthesize this feedback set into one product decision
turn survey comments into cited themes and risks
badExamples: collect private feedback from an unapproved system

goals.title: Competitor Evidence Brief
goals.summary: Compare a narrow competitor question using current, cited evidence and explicit uncertainty.
goals.defaultGoal: Research the supplied competitor question, compare only the dimensions that affect the decision, cite current primary evidence, and produce a concise brief that separates facts from inference.

requiredInputs.label: Decision question
requiredInputs.description: The specific product or positioning decision the comparison must inform.

requiredInputs.label: Comparison set
requiredInputs.description: The products or organizations in scope.

goals.sampleInput: Compare how three agent products explain proof and stopping conditions on their public product pages.
goals.expectedArtifact: An evidence-backed comparison brief with citations, inference labels, and a bounded recommendation.

criteria: Every time-sensitive claim links to a current primary source.
The comparison stays within the supplied decision question.
Facts, inference, missing evidence, and recommendation are clearly separated.

reliability.summary: An experimental research goal that requires current source access and careful citation review.

goodExamples: compare competitor onboarding flows with cited evidence
research how three products position verification
create a current competitor brief for this pricing decision

goals.title: Landing Page Message Pass
goals.summary: Turn approved evidence into one clear landing-page message direction and primary action.
goals.defaultGoal: Create three distinct landing-page message directions from the supplied audience, offer, and evidence, select the strongest direction using a clear rubric, and produce one review-ready concept without inventing claims.

requiredInputs.label: Audience
requiredInputs.description: The one audience this message should help.

requiredInputs.label: Offer
requiredInputs.description: The product or outcome being offered.

requiredInputs.label: Approved evidence
requiredInputs.description: Customer language, product proof, or constraints that claims may use.

goals.sampleInput: Explain Loop It to product builders who want a safe Codex prompt without learning terminal commands.
goals.expectedArtifact: Three message directions and one selected landing-page concept with assumptions marked.

criteria: The concept names one audience, one problem, and one clear promise.
Every factual claim is supported by supplied evidence or marked as an assumption.
A reviewer can identify the primary action without additional explanation.

reliability.summary: An experimental messaging goal that creates a local draft and requires human approval before publishing.

goodExamples: create three positioning concepts from this customer research
tighten the landing page message for one audience
turn these product facts into a reviewable value proposition

goals.title: Source-to-Content Pack
goals.summary: Repurpose one approved source into a small channel-ready content set without adding unsupported claims.
goals.defaultGoal: Turn the supplied source into a concise article summary, email draft, and social post, preserve the approved voice, and trace every factual claim back to the source.

requiredInputs.label: Approved source
requiredInputs.description: The document, transcript, or notes that contain the usable facts.

requiredInputs.label: Voice guidance
requiredInputs.description: Existing examples or constraints that define the intended tone.

goals.sampleInput: Repurpose the Loop It release explanation into a short product update, an email draft, and a LinkedIn draft.
goals.expectedArtifact: A three-format content pack with a source-claim checklist and no external publishing.

criteria: Every factual claim can be traced to the supplied source.
Each format follows its channel constraints without changing the core message.
All outputs remain drafts and clearly identify assumptions or missing evidence.

reliability.summary: A local content-drafting goal; publishing and outreach stay behind approval.

goodExamples: turn this article into a LinkedIn draft in our brand voice
repurpose this approved transcript into three content formats
create a content pack without inventing claims
badExamples: send the email to our list
make up statistics to improve the story

goals.title: Data Quality Snapshot
goals.summary: Measure the most important quality risks in a supplied dataset without modifying the source.
goals.defaultGoal: Inspect the supplied dataset without mutating it, quantify missing, duplicate, invalid, and suspicious values, and produce a prioritized remediation brief with reproducible counts.

requiredInputs.label: Dataset
requiredInputs.description: A local table, spreadsheet, or structured file safe to inspect.

requiredInputs.label: Quality rules
requiredInputs.description: Known required fields, valid ranges, uniqueness rules, or business invariants.

goals.sampleInput: Inspect a synthetic signup export for missing emails, duplicate IDs, invalid dates, and unexplained status values.
goals.expectedArtifact: A data-quality report with reconciled counts, prioritized risks, and no source mutation.

criteria: Row and column totals reconcile with the inspected source.
Every reported issue includes a count, rule, and representative evidence.
The source remains unchanged and remediation steps are prioritized by impact.

reliability.summary: A read-only data goal for bounded local files, not production databases.

goodExamples: audit this csv for missing and duplicate values
create a read-only quality snapshot for this spreadsheet
quantify the biggest data quality risks in this local export
badExamples: upload customer data to an external service

goals.title: Experiment Results Brief
goals.summary: Turn bounded experiment evidence into a decision brief without overstating what the data proves.
goals.defaultGoal: Analyze the supplied experiment results, verify the metric calculations, separate observed effects from interpretation, and recommend continue, revise, or stop with explicit uncertainty.

requiredInputs.label: Hypothesis
requiredInputs.description: The statement the experiment was designed to test.

requiredInputs.label: Results
requiredInputs.description: The local measurements, sample details, and known caveats.

goals.sampleInput: Review activation results for two onboarding variants and decide whether the evidence supports a wider rollout.
goals.expectedArtifact: A concise decision brief with verified metrics, caveats, and a bounded recommendation.

criteria: Reported metrics reconcile with the supplied results.
Observed effects, interpretation, and causal claims are clearly separated.
The recommendation names uncertainty, risk, and the next evidence needed.

reliability.summary: A bounded evaluation goal that does not replace statistical or domain review.

goodExamples: turn these ab test results into a cautious decision brief
verify the experiment metrics and recommend the next step
explain what this evaluation supports without overstating causality
badExamples: roll out the winning variant automatically

goals.title: SOP From Messy Notes
goals.summary: Turn incomplete process notes into a usable procedure with gaps and approval points exposed.
goals.defaultGoal: Turn the supplied process notes into a concise standard operating procedure, preserve unresolved gaps as questions, and make ownership, inputs, decisions, and completion evidence explicit.

requiredInputs.label: Process notes
requiredInputs.description: The approved local notes, transcript, or checklist describing the workflow.

requiredInputs.label: Operator
requiredInputs.description: The role expected to follow the finished procedure.

goals.sampleInput: Turn release handoff notes into an SOP for a maintainer preparing a plugin release for review.
goals.expectedArtifact: A review-ready SOP with prerequisites, steps, decision points, evidence, owners, and open questions.

criteria: The procedure identifies its operator, trigger, inputs, and completion evidence.
Decision points, approval gates, and failure paths are explicit.
Missing information remains visible as an open question instead of being invented.

reliability.summary: A documentation goal that requires an operator to review real-world accuracy.

goodExamples: turn these messy handoff notes into an sop
document this support escalation process with owners and gaps
convert this process transcript into a reviewable procedure
badExamples: send the SOP to external recipients

goals.title: Support Knowledge Gap Audit
goals.summary: Compare recurring support questions with existing guidance and prioritize one missing answer.
goals.defaultGoal: Compare the supplied support questions with the approved help content, identify the highest-impact knowledge gap, and draft one review-ready answer without contacting customers.

requiredInputs.label: Support questions
requiredInputs.description: A bounded, approved set of anonymized questions with stable IDs.

requiredInputs.label: Existing help content
requiredInputs.description: The approved documentation or FAQ set to compare against.

goals.sampleInput: Review anonymized questions about installing Loop It and compare them with the current setup guidance.
goals.expectedArtifact: A prioritized gap report and one draft knowledge-base answer with source references.

criteria: Every identified gap cites a supplied support item and the relevant existing guidance.
The selected gap is prioritized using frequency and user impact.
The drafted answer is accurate, actionable, and remains an internal review draft.

reliability.summary: A local support-content goal; it never sends replies or reads unapproved queues.

goodExamples: find the biggest gap between these support questions and our faq
audit help content coverage using anonymized tickets
draft one missing support answer without sending it
badExamples: read the live support queue without approval
